No Poverty.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to health or well-being.

Quality Education.reason: The course explicitly provides quality tertiary legal education (Target 4.3) and develops practical legal skills for employment (Target 4.4) through understanding arbitration law and practical exercises.

Gender Equality.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to water or sanitation.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course addresses arbitration disputes involving civil law and government procurement, which supports stable economic activities and productive enterprise, aligning with Target 8.3.

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's focus on arbitration for disputes under the Government Procurement Act directly supports the legal framework necessary for developing quality, reliable, and sustainable infrastructure (Target 9.1).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course introduces Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR), which can promote more accessible and equal access to justice, thereby reducing inequalities of outcome (Target 10.3).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable cities or communities.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to responsible consumption or production.

Climate Action.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to climate action.

Life Below Water.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to marine life or oceans.

Life on Land.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course's core focus on Arbitration Law and Alternative Dispute Resolution (ADR) directly promotes the rule of law, ensures equal access to justice (Target 16.3), and contributes to developing effective institutions (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: No explicit or implicit evidence in the course syllabus links to global partnerships or international cooperation for the SDGs.
